response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (GISCollective, Green Map) and proven low-latency systems work (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/sec at Sociomantic), but modern C++ experience is thin and dated — limited to a brief 2014-2015 stint at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis. Recent work has centered on TypeScript/Node.js product engineering rather than systems programming, and there is no direct routing or navigation guidance experience. Strong engineering fundamentals and adaptability partially offset the stack mismatch, making this a borderline case worth a technical screen only if the C++ bar can be ramped.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data platform experience as founder of GISCollective (open-source GIS used by Green Map)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience with async I/O, caching, and fibers at Sociomantic scale
Broad full-stack versatility with demonstrated willingness to learn new stacks and domains
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and nearly a decade old; no Rust experience listed
No direct routing, tiling, or navigation guidance system experience
Recent roles focus on web/product development in TypeScript, distant from systems-level pipeline work
